rulings.Case Title: RICHARD WAYNE KOZEL VS. AIRLINE COACH SERVICE, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO ADMISSION/INTERROGATORIES
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 3, 2018, Line 1, DEFENDANT AIRLINE COACH SERVICE, INC.'s MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO ADMISSION/INTERROGATORIES Defendant Airline Coach Service, Inc.'s motion to compel response to form interrogatories and for monetary sanctions is off calendar as to the motion to compel and granted as to monetary sanctions. The court adopts in full recommendations and reasons for the recommendation in the report of Judge Pro Tem Tom Cohen. rulings.Case Title: QIAN & NEMECEK LLP, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. PETER M. RADIN JR.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion By Qian & Nemecek Llp To Compel Peter M. Radin, Jr. To Provide Responses To First Set Of Post-Judgment Requests For Production And First Set Of Post-Judgment Interrogatories And For Sanctions In The Amount Of $7,647.35
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday,December 3, 2018, Line 1, PLAINTIFF QIAN & NEMECEK LLP's Motion To Compel Peter M. Radin, Jr. To Provide Responses To First Set Of Post-Judgment Requests For Production And First Set Of Post-Judgment Interrogatories And For Sanctions In The Amount Of $7,647.35. Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No opposition filed. Defendant to provide responses to Plaintiff's post-judgment discovery 15 days from the filing of the order. Sanctions in the amount of $2071.95 awarded to Plaintiff. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rkane1089@aol.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MINT COLLECTION -410-418 JESSIE STREET CONDOMINIUM VS. FIFTH HISTORIC PROPERTIES, LLC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Leave To Add Cause Of Action For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty And To Substitute Patrick Mcnerney As Doe 1
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 3, 2018, Line 2, PLAINTIFF MINT COLLECTION -410-418 JESSIE STREET CONDOMINIUM's Motion For Leave To Add Cause Of Action For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty And To Substitute Patrick Mcnerney As Doe 1 Plaintiff's motion for leave to add cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty and to substitute Patrick McNerney as Doe 1 is granted. Granting the motion comports with California's policy of liberally allowing amendments to pleadings absent a clear showing of prejudice to another party. Neither Mr. McNerney nor any other party has shown that he will be prejudiced by the granting of this motion. Because there is sufficient time to take discovery and for motion practice before trial, any claim of prejudice is unpersuasive. The arguments that the motion is a misuse of the Doe procedure and the proposed new claim lacks merit as a matter of law are rejected on this motion, but may be re-raised by appropriate motion or at trial. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: JASON ROUSSOS VS. POUR GUYS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 3, 2018, Line 14, DEFENDANT CMS SECURITY, INCORPORATED DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendant CMS Security, Incorporated's demurrer to the first, fourth, sixth, eighth, and tenth causes of action for negligence, respondeat superior, and negligent hiring, supervision, and training in plaintiff Jason Roussos' second amended complaint is overruled in its entirety. Per Southland Corp. v. Superior Court (1988) 203 Cal.App.3d 656, 662, in the absence of ownership or possession, the existence of a duty to protect from criminal acts of third parties "will depend upon the proprietor's actual or apparent control of the adjacent property." While taken in isolation allegations about a commercial benefit from use of adjacent property, a "minimal, neighborly maintenance" of the same or a configuration of proprietor's premises requiring invitees to cross a sidewalk would each be insufficient to show the requisite control, the second amended complaint's allegations, taken together and liberally construed, sufficiently support a duty of care based on Pour Guys' control over the property where Mr. Rousse was injured. Pour Guys allegedly used the 10 feet strip of sidewalk as an outdoor patio of its business establishment. Pour Guys allegedly was aware of its customers' regular use of the sidewalk for ordering and eating food and for walking between the drinks and food area. Pour Guys allegedly encouraged such use by the way it designed its establishment and derived a substantial commercial benefit from it. Pour Guys allegedly agreed to maintain and clean the sidewalk in its lease agreement. The altercation and plaintiff's stabbing allegedly occurred on this strip of sidewalk just outside the drinks area. The second amended complaint sufficiently alleges that defendant CMS Security had an affirmative duty to protect Pour Guy's patrons based on its agency relationship with Pour Guys and sufficiently alleges that CMS Security's breach of duty proximately caused Mr. Roussos's injuries. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the Mr. Roussos is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: LESLIE LAPAYOWKER VS. AIRBNB,INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Carlos Del Olmo'S Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Granting Reinstatement Of Default
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 3, 2018, Line 11, (1 of 2) Defendant Carlos Del Olmo'S Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Granting Reinstatement Of Default Defendant Carlos Del Olmo's motion for reconsideration of the September 27, 2018 order reinstating the default entered against him on February 13, 2018 is continued to January 17, 2019 to give the parties an opportunity to meet and confer and, if necessary, file supplemental papers regarding the amount Mr. Del Olmo will be required to pay plaintiff Leslie Lapayowker as a condition of setting aside the September 27, 2018 order. The court finds that there is sufficient change of circumstances since the September 27, 2018 order was issued to warrant reconsideration per CCP 1008. Alternatively, just as the court did with regard to Ms. Lapayowker's motion for reconsideration of the March 27, 2018 order setting aside the default entered against Mr. Del Olmo, the court treats this motion as an invitation to reconsider per Le Francois v. Goel (2005) 35 Cal. 4th 1094 without regard to compliance with the requirements of CCP 100. Whether per CCP 1008 or the court's inherent authority, the court intends to vacate the September 27, 2018 order per CCP 473(b) on the condition that Mr. Del Olmo pay Ms. Lapayowker no later than 60 days after the entry of an order vacating the September 27, 2018 order all fees and costs Ms. Lapayowker incurred for: a) serving Mr. Del Olmo with the summons and complaint, b) opposing Mr. Del Olmo's motion that led to the March 27, 2018 order, c) filing Ms. Lapayowker's motion that led to the September 27, 2018 order, and d) opposing this motion. Considering all the evidence submitted on the prior motions and this motion, Mr. Del Olmo has shown sufficient facts to warrant the vacating of the default entered against him based on surprise, mistake, inadvertence and excusable neglect. Only "very slight evidence" is required to support a motion to set aside a default when the motion is promptly filed (as was the February 22, 2018 motion filed by Mr. Del Olmo) and the plaintiff has no credible claim of legal prejudice if the motion is granted apart from fees and costs incurred relating to the default. (Shamblin v. Brattain (1988) 44 Cal. 3d 474, 478). Setting aside the default comports with California's strong policy of deciding cases on the merits. Moreover, per the unique facts of this case, maintaining the default and allowing a potentially large default judgment including punitive damages to be entered against Mr. Del Olmo is arguably violative of his federal and California due process rights since it is undisputed that at all times from the filing of the February 13, 2018 request for entry of default Mr. Del Olmo (through counsel) has made clear to Ms. Lapayowker that it was his desire and intent to oppose this lawsuit on the merits and he had contacted attorneys to do so. Neither Hearn v. Howard (2009) 177 Cal. App. 4th 1193 nor any of the other authorities cited by Ms. Lapayowker (nor any authorities known to the court) preclude vacating the default entered against Mr. Del Olmo. Hearn is distinguishable in many important respects, not the least of which are that the appellant in that case was an attorney and the opposing counsel had recommended the filing of a motion to vacate the default which was ignored by appellant. Moreover, the trial court's decision to deny a motion to vacate the default was upheld on appeal based on an abuse of discretion standard and nothing in Hearn precludes this court from exercising its discretion to vacate the default entered against Mr. Del Olmo. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: LESLIE LAPAYOWKER VS. AIRBNB,INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: (1 Of 2) Defendant Carlos Del Olmo'S Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Granting Reinstatement Of Default (For Tentative Ruling Only)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 3, 2018, Line 11, (2 Of 2) Defendant Carlos Del Olmo'S Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Granting Reinstatement Of Default (For Tentative Ruling Only) While California policy, the interests of justice, and very possibly due process support vacating the default entered against Mr. Del Olmo in the exercise of the court's discretion per CCP 473(b), that subsection provides discretion for the court to do so "upon any terms as may be just." Justice in this case mandates that Mr. Del Olmo, not Ms. Lapayowker, bear the monetary burden of Mr. Del Olmo's now acknowledged falsehoods and other misconduct. To facilitate arriving at an accurate number to be paid by Mr. Del Olmo, no later than December 14, 2018 counsel for Ms. Lapayowker must send an accounting with supporting documentation of all monies claimed by Ms. Lapayowker consistent with this order. No later than January 4, 2019 the parties are required to meet and confer regarding the amount claimed by Ms. Lapayowker in an effort to reach agreement on a number. If the parties are able to reach agreement, counsel for Ms. Lapayowker should so inform the court by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to counsel for Mr. Del Olmo. If the parties are unable to reach an agreement, no later than January 11, 2019 each side may file supplemental papers limited to the issue of the amount that Mr. Del Olmo should be required to pay Ms. Lapayowker. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Del Olmo is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. (end of tentative ruling) =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: HANSON BRIDGETT LLP VS. PHILIP ACHILLES
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 3, 2018, Line 6, PLAINTIFF HANSON BRIDGETT LLP's MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION Petitioner Hanson Bridgett LLP's petition to confirm arbitration award is granted. Even were the court to treat to the answer filed by respondent Philip Achilles as a timely rejection of the arbitration award, Mr. Achilles failed to satisfy his burden per Business and Professions Code section 6204(a) that his failure to appear at the arbitration hearing was not willful. Mr. Attridge's email and the declarations of Mr. Achilles and Mr. Attridge show that Mr. Achilles knowingly chose not to attend the arbitration because he did not believe that it would have any utility. That is willful non-appearance per section 6204(a). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Hanson Bridgett is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: ANNA GATTI VS. LOOP AI LAB INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs Following Successful Anti-Slapp Motion Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 425.16
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 3, 2018, Line 7, PLAINTIFF ANNA GATTI's Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs Following Successful Anti-Slapp Motion Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 425.16 Cross-defendant Anna Gatti's motion for attorney's fees and costs per CCP 425.16 is granted in small part. Ms. Gatti is awarded fees and costs of $20,688.70, comprised of $20,600 in fees and $88.70 in costs, against cross-complainant Loop A1 Labs Inc. Ms. Gatti is entitled to a fee award for her successful striking of Loop's cross-claim for malicious prosecution. That award is limited to fees that were reasonably incurred. Based on the court's own experience and reviewing all the papers filed on the anti-SLAPP motion and this motion, the hourly rate of $515 for the work performed by counsel for Ms. Gatti is at the high end of the range of a reasonable hourly rate for the counsel who performed the work and thus is accepted by the court. However, the hours claimed by counsel for Ms. Gatti, while undoubtedly spent by counsel, far exceed what is reasonable for either the anti-SLAPP motion or this motion. Based on the court's own experience and reviewing all the papers filed on the anti-SLAPP motion and this motion, the high end of the range of a reasonable number of hours for each motion was 20 hours. The malicious prosecution claim was a classic SLAPP and it took very little effort to point that out. The first prong of the anti-SLAPP analysis was easily satisfied since a malicious prosecution claim always or nearly so arises from protected petitioning activity and that was surely the case here. The second prong was also easily satisfied since the express language of the federal judgment showed that Ms. Gatti had not abandoned her federal court counterclaim and thus as a matter law Loop could not show that the counterclaim had been resolved in its favor. Any reasonable attorney who justifiably claims an hourly rate exceeding $500 should have been able to make these two simple points in far fewer than twenty hours and certainly no more than twenty hours, including both moving and reply papers and attendance at a hearing. This motion too is quite simple because of the statutory entitlement to fees and the uncomplicated procedural and factual circumstances giving rise to the motion. Any reasonable attorney who justifiably claims an hourly rate exceeding $500 should have been able to establish Ms. Gatti's entitlement to fees and present a lodestar analysis in far fewer than twenty hours and certainly no more than twenty hours, including both moving and reply papers and attendance at a hearing. Because both the hourly rate and the hours spent are at the high end of the reasonable range and there are no special circumstances warranting a multiplier, no multiplier is awarded. While some of Loop's arguments in opposition to this motion were clearly frivolous, the court declines to engage in any CCP 128.5 inquiry because to do so only creates further collateral litigation in this already vastly over-litigated case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Ms. Brayer is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: RONALD P. VAN SIEGMAN VS. FCA US, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set One
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 3, 2018, Line 4, PLAINTIFF RONALD VAN SIEGMAN'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set One. Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No courtesy copies of moving or reply papers provided by Plaintiff. If Plaintiff provide courtesy copies to Dept. 301 by December 11, 2018 (with notice of such to counsel for Defendant) the matter will be continued to December 21, 2018, otherwise the matter is off calendar. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rkane1089@aol.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SULAIMAN AKRAMI VS. UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Travis Alexander Thorntons Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 3, 2018, Line 9, DEFENDANT TRAVIS THORNTON Defendant Travis Alexander Thorntons' Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities Defendant Travis Thornton's motion to strike the punitive damages allegations and prayer against him in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Sulaiman Akrami is granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Thornton is required to prepare a proposed order which states that the motion was not opposed and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: MIDLAND FUNDING LLC VS. KEITH HERMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 3, 2018, Line 10, PLAINTIFF MIDLAND FUNDING LLC's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings Plaintiff Midland Funding LLC's motion for judgment on the pleadings is granted. No opposition filed. Midland is entitled to judgment in its favor in the principal amount of $3,741.08 plus costs of $330.50 against defendant Keith Herman. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for Midland is required to prepare a proposed order which states that the motion was not opposed and a proposed judgment and must bring them to the hearing or email them to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: LOUIS NG VS. HEE SHUN HUDSON LI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Louis Ng'S Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 3, 2018, Line 12, Plaintiff Louis Ng'S Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment Plaintiff Louis Ng's application for a right to attach order and writ of attachment is off calendar without prejudice to re-noticing the application upon proof of proper service. Substituted service on a security guard is not proper service, except where the party sought to be served lives or works in a gate community and the guard refuses entry to a licensed process server. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =302/hk
